title: API Returning 500 Errors — OAuth Users

slack.message: Getting 500 errors from enterprise customers — anyone else seeing this?
Latency looks normal to me on Datadog? P99 is 180ms
DB connections slightly elevated but nothing crazy, 23-31% utilization
Wait — errors are actually 401s not 500s. Only OAuth users are affected

logs.message: OAuth token signature verification failed: algorithm mismatch HS256 != RS256
OAuth token signature verification failed: algorithm mismatch HS256 != RS256
401 Unauthorized — rejected 847 requests in last 60s from OAuth users

github.author: dev-alice
github.message: feat: update OAuth token validation to use RS256 algorithm

red_herrings: PagerDuty alert says latency spike, but overall latency is normal — only OAuth auth path is slow
DB connections at 23-31% look suspicious but are nowhere near exhaustion threshold of 100%
correct_root_cause: Deploy at 12:00 changed OAuth token validation from HS256 to RS256 algorithm. Existing OAuth tokens were signed with HS256 — they started failing validation 2 hours later when user sessions required re-authentication. The algorithm mismatch caused auth latency to spike to 850ms and generated 401 errors exclusively for OAuth users.

expected_hypothesis_sequence.hypothesis: Database connection pool exhaustion causing timeouts
expected_hypothesis_sequence.rejection_evidence: DB connections at 23-31% capacity. Exhaustion threshold is 100%. CPU at 15-22% — no connection storm signature. Evidence is unambiguous: DB is healthy.

expected_hypothesis_sequence.hypothesis: Third-party OAuth provider degradation or outage
expected_hypothesis_sequence.rejection_evidence: Auth logs show 'algorithm mismatch HS256 != RS256' — not a connectivity failure or provider timeout. This is a local validation error, not an upstream dependency issue.

expected_hypothesis_sequence.hypothesis: Recent auth deploy broke OAuth token validation algorithm
expected_hypothesis_sequence.confirming_evidence: GitHub deploy at 12:00 changed validation from HS256 to RS256. Auth latency spiked to 850ms at 14:02 — two hours post-deploy when OAuth sessions expired and re-authenticated. Error pattern 'algorithm mismatch' confirms existing HS256 tokens fail against the new RS256 validator. 401 errors affect only OAuth users, not session-based users.

action_items: Revert auth middleware to HS256 or run token migration script to re-sign existing OAuth tokens as RS256
Add pre-deploy token compatibility check to CI pipeline — fail build if algorithm changes would invalidate live tokens
Add OAuth-specific error rate monitoring separate from general API error rate
Document OAuth token algorithm migration runbook with rollback procedure
Add canary deploy gate for auth service changes — route 1% of OAuth traffic before full rollout
detection_gap: Standard error rate monitoring did not distinguish OAuth from non-OAuth users. PagerDuty alert fired on latency (misleading) not error type. OAuth-specific 401 errors were invisible in aggregated dashboards for 19 minutes.
